prompt: Japanese ukiyo-e woodblock triptych, Edo period (1800s), in the manner of Hiroshige's travel series. Three sequential panels showing a lone traveler searching for a single destination marker. Left panel: the traveler walks a narrow mountain path — the destination stone is nearby and clearly visible. Centre panel: the traveler crosses a wide flat plain — the destination is a small mark far across the field, the surrounding space vast and empty. Right panel: the traveler stands amid an enormous mountainous landscape of peaks and valleys — the destination is invisible, the figure reduced to a tiny speck swallowed by exponentially expanding empty space. Flat unmodulated color fields throughout — indigo, pale ochre, cream, and black only. Bold even-weight black outlines. High diagonal vantage point with flattened perspective. Visible woodblock grain texture in sky and open ground areas. Mist and empty negative space used compositionally. No photographic shading, no gradients, no color blending, no Western perspective techniques, no text, no labels, no anachronistic elements, no modern objects.
model: DALL-E 3 via OpenAI (ChatGPT Plus) OR Banana (Gemini Imagen)
tradition_approach: Targeted Hiroshige's triptych travel format, high diagonal vantage, flat unmodulated indigo-ochre-cream color fields, bold even-weight outlines, and mist as compositional negative space — with exponentially expanding empty space across three panels as the structural vehicle for the curse of dimensionality
